description: CPT 59400 (routine obstetric care incl. vaginal delivery) is valid only for female patients.
source: CMS NCCI Policy Manual 2024; CPT 2024 (synthetic simplification)

description: CPT 59400 must be billed with a pregnancy/delivery diagnosis (ICD-10 O80.x, Z37.0, or Z34.x).
Hard policy cap: billed amount for CPT 59400 may not exceed $4,000.
Screening colonoscopy (CPT 45378) is guideline-recommended at age 45+. Younger patients require a documented diagnostic indication and should be reviewed.
CPT 45378 (diagnostic colonoscopy) must carry a GI-related diagnosis (screening, polyp, diverticulosis, bleeding, etc.).
Hard policy cap: billed amount for CPT 45378 may not exceed $1,800.
Hard policy cap: billed amount for CPT 99213 (office visit, low complexity) may not exceed $200.
Hard policy cap: billed amount for CPT 99214 (office visit, moderate complexity) may not exceed $350.
CPT 93000 (ECG) must carry a cardiac-related diagnosis.
Hard policy cap: billed amount for CPT 93000 may not exceed $150.
CPT 70450 (CT head without contrast) must carry a head/neuro-related diagnosis (headache, syncope, head injury, etc.).
Hard policy cap: billed amount for CPT 70450 may not exceed $1,500.
Total knee arthroplasty (CPT 27447) in patients under 40 is uncommon and should be reviewed for medical necessity.
CPT 27447 (total knee arthroplasty) must carry a knee osteoarthritis diagnosis (ICD-10 M17.x).
Hard policy cap: billed amount for CPT 27447 may not exceed $50,000. NOTE: this is a generous hard ceiling; the statistical amount_anomaly check is a separate, tighter signal.
